name: Myrtle Point Fun Bus Inc
service_types: community/recreation shuttle for kids and seniors
districts_or_customers: takes kids to swimming lessons (Coquille pool), league sports games, Junior Olympics; also adult/senior outings
recent_news: Donated/replaced bus in 2004 with funding from Myrtle Point Rotary Club ($35K) and Fun Bus ($5K) to buy a newer bus from Bandon Dunes Resort; profiled by The World as a '40 stories' feature.
fit_notes: Not a fit for Summit. Myrtle Point Fun Bus Inc. is a small community nonprofit run by volunteers providing recreation/senior transport in Coos County, OR — not a school-district yellow-bus contractor. No fleet, no contracts, no commercial economics.

name: Island Bus Co
service_types: K-12 school bus contractor (Sitka School District)
districts_or_customers: Sitka School District (sole school bus provider for Sitka)
recent_news: 2022 KCAW: Trudy Prewitt confirmed her drivers were covering all Sitka school routes despite a national driver shortage. Sitka Tours/Prewitt Enterprises has now operated >60 years (founded 1964 by Gene Prewitt); Trudy has publicly referenced 50 years of personal involvement, signaling clear succession-risk.
fit_notes: Interesting but niche: sole Sitka school-district contractor combined with a cruise/tour business under one family (Prewitt). High succession signal (60+ year-old family business, long-tenured owner). Very small scale and remote Alaska location reduce platform synergy; could be acquired as a defensive AK foothold but is likely sub-scale for Summit/I Squared platform criteria.

name: JCR Transportation Inc
service_types: school bus / student transport (Cape Cod area)
likely small-vehicle SPED runs given fleet size
districts_or_customers: Cape Cod districts (Bourne/Falmouth area implied by addresses; not publicly confirmed)
recent_news: No public M&A or news identified; operating address split between Bourne (10 Millennium Dr) and East Falmouth (38 Red Brook Rd); maintains a Facebook page but no website.
fit_notes: Plausible small independent Cape Cod operator; very limited public footprint. Likely sub-scale (~22 employees, ~$0.9M est revenue) and probably a small-vehicle SPED/in-district contractor rather than a big yellow-bus operator. Worth a quick outreach call to confirm services and ownership before further work.

name: Mommy Drives Inc
service_types: small-vehicle student/child transportation (likely alternative student transport / rideshare-for-kids style, not yellow bus)
districts_or_customers: Greater Grand Rapids / Kentwood MI area (not publicly identified)
recent_news: No public news or M&A identified; only listing found is a Manta.com business directory entry as a ground transportation company in Kentwood, MI.
fit_notes: Unclear fit. Name and lack of public presence suggest a small alternative-student-transport / child-courier operation (more HopSkipDrive-style than yellow bus). Without confirmation that it runs a school-district contract and a real bus fleet, this is not a near-term tuck-in target for Summit. Tag for phone confirmation only.

name: Access Transportation Sltns
service_types: likely paratransit / accessible / non-emergency medical and possibly SPED student transport
districts_or_customers: Commerce City / Adams County, CO area
recent_news: No M&A or operational news identified; only directory listings (b2byellowpages, etc.). Adams County's senior/disabled Community Transit is a separate program — no confirmed relationship to Access Transportation Solutions.
fit_notes: Profile (name 'Access', Commerce City CO, ~20 employees, accessible vehicles) reads like a paratransit/NEMT/SPED van operator rather than a true yellow-bus school contractor. Low priority for Summit tuck-in unless confirmed to hold meaningful district SPED contracts; needs outbound call to qualify.
